rationale: Palette clash is severe: the archetype's pastel pink/baby blue kitchen aesthetic directly conflicts with Bounce Blast's dark moody purple + amber UI, so a literal port would feel off-brand. Mechanically, "pouring into a jar" is a weak analog for the game's tap-to-place + physics chain-reaction loop — there's no block placement, no bounce payoff, and no level-clear moment to anchor the satisfaction beat. Only worth pursuing as a loose inspiration: borrow the ASMR pour cadence and apply it to balls cascading into a grid, but don't commission this archetype as-is (also note n=1 advertiser, low velocity 0.21 — weak signal).
